name: Lenovo Tab P12 Pro
introText: The Lenovo Tab P12 Pro is a 12.6-inch AMOLED 120Hz tablet with Snapdragon 870 and an included stylus pen for $649 CAD — the best large-format Android tablet under $700.
whatWorks: 12.6-inch AMOLED 120Hz display at $649 is extraordinary value — this display size and quality typically costs $1,000+. The screen alone makes it compelling for media consumption.
256GB storage and 8GB RAM included — more storage and RAM than any competing tablet at this price, with no need to pay for upgrades.
worthKnowing: Snapdragon 870 is a 2021 flagship chip — still capable but showing age vs. current Snapdragon 8 Gen 3. Gaming and demanding apps will perform noticeably slower than Tab S10+.
Lenovo's software support track record is weaker than Samsung or Apple — fewer guaranteed years of OS updates and a simpler software ecosystem.

commonComplaints.title: Older Snapdragon 870 shows in demanding tasks
commonComplaints.body: The 870 chip is fine for media and productivity but stutters in 3D gaming and heavy multitasking compared to 2024 flagships.

commonComplaints.title: Software support uncertainty
commonComplaints.body: Lenovo doesn't guarantee the same multi-year update commitment as Samsung or Apple. Long-term support is uncertain.

commonComplaints.title: Stylus is included but basic
commonComplaints.body: The bundled stylus works for basic annotation but lacks the pressure sensitivity and palm rejection quality of Apple Pencil or Samsung S Pen Pro.

verdict: The Lenovo Tab P12 Pro is the best Android tablet under $700 for media consumption — the 12.6-inch AMOLED display is the headline feature at this price. For productivity and long-term support, the Samsung Galaxy Tab S10 FE at $599 with S Pen is often a better all-around value.

realBuyers.quote: Bought for video editing reviews and streaming. The AMOLED screen is incredible for the price. Better display than my previous tablet that cost twice as much.
Great for movies and YouTube. 12.6 inches is big and the AMOLED makes it gorgeous.
Good value but Snapdragon 870 is showing age in newer games. Fine for most things.
